name: Free-for-All Berlin Absurd Cartoon
line_style: Bold thick black outlines, oversized heads, compact rubber-hose bodies, huge readable facial reactions, ugly-funny silhouettes, bent props, warped Berlin architecture and simple repeatable cartoon shapes. The target is Free-for-All-level exaggeration energy, not realism and not a direct copy of any existing show.
exaggeration_rules: Push every normal Berlin situation 30 percent more cartoon than feels realistic.
Rico's eyes can become huge blank panic circles; Falk's smile can become rubbery and too wide.
Buildings may lean, doors may look judgmental, mailboxes may look like broken teeth and club queues may bend like a snake.
Hands, noses, eyebrows and posture carry the joke before background detail does.
Every production panel must remain readable with 1 main gag, 1 clear emotion and 1 clear location.
Use bright aggressive accent colors against dirty Berlin neutrals; never let the frame become gray-realistic.
real_world_reference_rules: Berghain, KitKat, Christopher Street Day and Loveparade can exist as Berlin reference points in scripts and episode planning.
Generated images must not copy real club logos, exact facades, interiors, event branding, posters or copyrighted design language.
Club Nein remains the fictional club container for Berghain-style gatekeeping satire.
KitKat-related jokes should play as Rico's innocent misunderstanding and Berlin culture shock, not explicit sexual imagery.
Christopher Street Day scenes should be colorful, crowded and joyful-chaotic without mocking queer people.
Loveparade references belong mostly to DJ Krätze's unreliable old rave legends and exaggerated flashbacks.
character_rules: Every recurring character must have an instantly readable silhouette.
Rico must look young, clean, sheltered and too harmless for Berlin.
Falk must look soft, moral, smug and Prenzlauer-Berg comfortable, never like a classic villain.
Sven Null must look like a silent wall, not like an action bouncer.
DJ Nebel must look pretentious, strange and spiritual, not like a cool superstar.
The Görli cats stay physically cat-like but pose and talk like tiny Kiez clan bosses.
Facial expressions are exaggerated, loud and cartoonish, never subtle realism.
No character should look like a realistic portrait; reduce them into repeatable icons with big shapes.
background_rules: Haus Nebenwirkung looks like a chaotic Berlin microcosm: graffiti, stickers, broken mailboxes, trash cans, old flyers, mold, cables, Späti neon and political leftovers.
Club Nein is a fictional club world: dark door, queue silhouettes, red light, fog, no real club logos and no readable branding.
Prenzlauer Berg scenes must contrast the house: clean floors, plants, Hafermilch, Siebträger, Naturwein, yoga calm and polished moral comfort.
The Späti is a social control room, not just a shop: shelves, cheap coffee, Pfand, gossip and deadpan observation.
Backgrounds support one clear joke per frame. Do not make normal production panels as overloaded as pitch posters.
Berlin must feel like a warped cartoon pressure cooker, not a photographed city.
camera_rules: Default to vertical 9:16 framing for short-form output, but keep compositions readable as comic panels.
Every panel must work as a clean frame without speech bubbles, readable dialogue text or fake lettering.
Use close-ups for Rico's innocence, Falk's slippery moral smile and the cats' gangster commentary.
Use medium shots for fee conversations, house hallway reveals, Späti reality checks and Club Nein door rejection.
Avoid huge crowds or complex physical interactions unless split into simpler shots.
negative_rules: No realistic German TV drama look.
No glossy Pixar, Disney or 3D render look.
No dark realistic graphic novel look.
No readable dialogue text inside generated images.
No fake lettering or fake subtitles inside generated images.
No direct copy of existing IP, clubs, series, artists or characters.
No real drug-use instruction or glamorization.
No random outfit changes for recurring characters.
No normal cute house cats when the shot needs Görli-Clan energy.
